title: 10 best herbal essential oils for swollen gums
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for swollen gums are a natural and effective remedy to alleviate discomfort, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. These oils work by penetrating deep into the gum tissue, reducing swelling and pain caused by various factors such as gingivitis, periodontal disease, or mouth sores. For instance, peppermint oil's antibacterial properties combat bacteria that contribute to swollen gums, while tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and discomfort. Using these oils in a carrier lotion or mouthwash can improve daily lives by providing instant relief from painful gum issues, allowing individuals to eat and socialize with confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease pain. The oil's antimicrobial properties also combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation, while its antiseptic properties soothe the affected area, promoting a healthy healing process. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils contain compounds that stimulate blood flow, helping to flush out toxins and reduce swelling. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for alleviating swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, reducing swelling and inflammation associated with gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eucalyptol, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain by decreasing blood flow to the affected area and reducing pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Analgesic properties: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil contains analgesic compounds that help to numb the pain associated with swollen gums, providing relief from discomfort and making it easier to eat and speak.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums.
Barks: Containing anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, barks help combat bacterial infections and soothe gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves at dawn in dry conditions and store them in an airtight container.
Steam distill 50g of eucalyptus globulus leaves with 100ml of water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it using activated charcoal to remove impurities.
Bottle the filtered oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it away from direct sunlight.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before using it as a natural remedy for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. The oil's antiseptic qualities also help reduce redness and swelling by soothing the affected area. Additionally, tea tree essential oils contain anti-inflammatory compounds that ease discomfort and pain associated with swollen gums. By incorporating this oil into your oral care routine, you can experience a reduction in inflammation and promote overall gum health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Tea Tree Essential Oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to swollen gums (gingivitis) by reducing inflammation and infection.
Anti-inflammatory: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it a natural remedy for alleviating discomfort associated with swollen gums.
Antioxidant: Tea Tree Essential Oil contains antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may contribute to gum disease and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antibacterial compounds that help combat gum inflammation and reduce swelling.
Bark: Contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a still for 4-6 hours, collecting the resulting oil.
Filter the collected oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Store the prepared oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keeping it away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain. The oil's compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause inflammation in the mouth. Additionally, ginger has natural antibacterial properties, helping to combat gum infections that can contribute to swollen gums. By applying ginger essential oils topically or inhaling its vapors, individuals can experience relief from swollen and inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger essential oil reduces inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, providing relief from swollen gums.

Antimicrobial: Herbal ginger essential oil possesses antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for gum infections and inflammation, promoting healthy gums.
Analgesic: Ginger essential oil's analgesic properties provide pain relief by reducing discomfort and soreness associated with swollen gums, making it an effective natural remedy for oral health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, the underground stem of the ginger plant, contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums.
Roots: Ginger roots are a rich source of bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties to combat gum swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested zingiber officinale rhizomes in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Steam distill 20 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizome in 500 milliliters of water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Bottle and store the resulting ginger essential oil in an amber-colored glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce swelling and ease pain by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial properties help combat gum infections, which can contribute to gum inflammation. As a result, English lavender essential oils can provide relief from swollen gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender oil's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation.
Wound-healing properties: Lavender oil's ability to promote wound healing accelerates the recovery process for swollen gums, helping to repair damaged tissue and restore oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used to create a calming and anti-inflammatory essential oil that helps to reduce swelling in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers of the English lavender plant are used to produce an antiseptic and soothing essential oil that helps to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c for 30 minutes to obtain the oil.
Combine 5 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia leaves and 50 ml of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow infusion and extraction.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids before bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the mouth. When applied topically to the affected area, it can help to soothe and calm swollen gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, thyme essential oils' antimicrobial properties can also help to prevent infection and promote healing of the gums.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Herbal thyme essential oil has antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, which can cause swollen gums, by killing bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans and Escherichia coli.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal thyme essential oil help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant property: Herbal thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, ultimately reducing the risk of gum disease and swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used to create essential oils that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Buds: Thyme buds contain potent antimicrobial compounds that inhibit bacterial growth, making them effective in treating gum infections and reducing swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at dawn for maximum potency.
Wash the harvested plant material with distilled water to remove impurities and debris gently.
Steam distill the washed plant material using a clevenger apparatus or equivalent equipment patiently.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass vial after 2-3 hours of distillation carefully.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, peppermint oil reduces inflammation and swelling by constricting blood vessels, which in turn alleviates pain and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's analgesic and antiseptic properties help to soothe the gum tissue, reducing redness and sensitivity. By using herbal peppermint essential oils regularly, individuals can experience relief from swollen gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in swollen gums, making it an effective remedy for gingivitis and other gum-related issues.

Antibacterial properties: Peppermint essential oil contains antibacterial agents that can help combat the underlying bacterial infections that cause swollen gums, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.
Anesthetic properties: The cooling sensation of peppermint essential oil can numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort caused by swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to numb and reduce pain in swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: Peppermint rhizomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the mouth.
Barks: The barks of peppermint contain flavonoids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective against gum infections and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh peppermint leaves from organic sources in the morning.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of cold water for 10 seconds.
Steam distill the washed peppermint leaves with 200 milliliters of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 60 minutes.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol by filtering it through a coffee filter paper and collecting the clear liquid.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an airtight lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort. The oil's natural analgesic qualities also ease pain and soothe gum irritation, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gum inflammation, thereby supporting overall oral health and preventing further issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation, which helps alleviate swelling in the gums and relieves pain associated with gum inflammation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of Ceylon cinnamon essential oil help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy tissue growth.

Natural antibacterial property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has been found to possess natural antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, such as periodontitis, which can cause swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, the roots help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause swollen gums.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe swollen gum tissues and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinnamomum verum leaves from mature trees at dawn when the dew is still present for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and dry place for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release essential oils and flavor compounds.
Steam distill the crushed cinnamomum verum leaves in a copper still with water at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 hours to extract oil.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to separate essential oil from impurities and water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains methyl salicylate, a natural derivative of aspirin that effectively reduces swelling and relieves pain in the gums. When applied topically to the affected area, wintergreen oil can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, providing rapid relief from discomfort and inflammation. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, promoting healthy oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: Wintergreen oil contains high concentrations of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.

Antibacterial Properties: The oil's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Wintergreen essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor and menthol, which help to reduce inflammation in the gums and alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wintergreen plant contain high levels of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that helps reduce swelling and ease discomfort in gum inflammation.

Barks: Wintergreen barks have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including gum infections and mouth sores, due to their antimicrobial properties and ability to soothe irritated tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the early morning dew when fragrant.
Cleanly wash the harvested plant material with distilled water and gently pat dry with paper towels.
Steam distill 100g of the cleaned gaultheria procumbens plant material for 30 minutes using a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass vial, allowing it to settle for at least 2 hours before separation from water.
Filter the settled essential oil through a coffee filter or fine-mesh cloth and store in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, sage oil reduces swelling and relieves pain by constricting blood vessels and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes. Its antiseptic and antibacterial properties also help combat oral bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy environment for the gums to heal. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties help reduce plaque and tartar buildup, further alleviating swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Sage oil's antibacterial properties help combat bacteria that cause gum inflammation, reducing swelling and pain.
Anti-inflammatory property: Sage oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing.
Antioxidant property: Sage oil's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting healthy gum cell growth and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including gum inflammation, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them in the shade for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 500g of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 1 liter of water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and sedimentation particles within 15 minutes.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles, store them in a cool place for up to 1 year, and label with the date of preparation within 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain. The oil's anethole compound has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy gum environment. Additionally, fennel's antiseptic properties help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate gum issues. By applying fennel essential oils topically or inhaling its vapors, individuals can experience relief from swollen gums and maintain a healthier oral cavity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, allowing for quicker healing and relief.
Antimicrobial properties: Fennel essential oil's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease and infection, reducing the severity of swollen gums.
Analgesic properties: Fennel essential oil's analgesic properties help reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums, making it a natural remedy for alleviating dental pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain compounds that have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against gum infections that cause swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare seeds at the peak of potency for maximum therapeutic benefits.
Combine 250 grams of harvested seeds with 1000 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the seed and carrier oil mixture in a warm dark place for four to six hours allowing for optimal infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the final foeniculum vulgare essential oil in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and refrigerate at 2 to 8 degrees celsius for up to six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal essential oils is by combining a few carefully selected oils. Mix equal parts of tea tree oil and peppermint oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the mixture to the affected area using a cotton swab twice daily. This blend provides antibacterial properties from tea tree oil and anti-inflammatory benefits from peppermint oil, promoting healthy gum healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal essential oils are various oral health issues, including mouth sores, toothaches, and bad breath. Certain essential oils like tea tree oil, peppermint oil, and eucalyptus oil have antimicrobial properties that help alleviate these problems by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and freshening the breath.
